title: lobelia leschenaultiana
intro: Lobelia leschenaultiana, also known as Indian tobacco or Kamala, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant to help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant contains compounds like lobeline, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially providing health benefits like reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Lobelia leschenaultiana is often prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
